REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CINDY PON, ANGELA PON, WARREN W.K. PON, AND WARREN PON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION THE MOTION IS DENIED AS DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHIFT THEIR BURDEN. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO ADDRESS ONE OF THE BASIS FOR THESE CAUSES OF ACTION - HABITABILITY; A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MAY NOT BE GRANTED IF IT DOES NOT DISPOSE OF THE ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION THE MOTION IS DENIED AS DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHIFT THEIR BURDEN AND SHOW THAT THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THEIR REASONABLE GROUND TO BELIEVE THAT CINDY PON WAS "AN OWNER OF RECORD." CINDY PON ATTEMPTS TO SHOW THAT SHE WAS "AN OWNER" (EQUITABLE), BUT DOES NOT PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE OR ARGUMENT THAT AS A MATTER OF LAW SHE HAD REASONABLE GROUNDS TO BELIEVE THAT SHE WAS "AN OWNER OF RECORD." PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CINDY PON, ANGELA PON, WARREN W.K. PON, AND WARREN PON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: J.H. Bornstein (415.235.8231) for defendants and moving parties, appearing with co-counsel (no card provided); Benjamin Humphreys of Humphreys Joiner Law Group (415.528.2929) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. As to the second and third causes of action the motion is denied as Defendants failed to shift their burden. Defendants fail to address one of the basis for these causes of action - habitability; a motion for summary adjudication may not be granted if it does not dispose of the entire cause of action. As to the fourth cause of action the motion is DENIED as Defendants failed to shift their burden and show that there is no triable issue of fact regarding their reasonable ground to believe that Cindy Pon was "an owner of record." Cindy Pon attempts to show that she was "an owner" (equitable), but does not provide any evidence or argument that as a matter of law she had reasonable grounds to believe that she was "an owner of record." Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

Notice Of Plaintiff Kami Kings Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Benjamin Humphrey, Esq. (415-528-2929) for Plaintiff/Moving party KAMI KING.
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq.(415-235-8231) for Defendant/opposing party Cindy Pon.

The Court announces the Tentative ruling. There is no authority in the Opposition, appeal is frivolous, attorney's fees on appeal is reasonable. ARGUED. Defendant Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAMI KING's Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal is GRANTED as requested in the amount of $8,365. See Carpenter v. Jack in the Box (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 454. Defendant to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-29-2015 Continued To Oct-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert F. Cohen, Esq. (800-492-2657), SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR Cindy Pon and Angela Pon, Defendants and moving parties.
Benjamin Humphreys, Esq. of Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt, (415-391-6496), for Kami King, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Court denies the plaintiff's request additional time to file Reply Brief. Arguments on the motion and sanctions are heard. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs CINDY PON and ANGELA PON's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Defendants fail to meet their burden of showing that the gravamen of Plaintiff's complaint is targeted at a protected activity. Defendants allege the complaint was filed in retaliation for an unlawful detainer action and judgment, but no action or judgment was ever filed. Defendants provide no support for their theory that this lawsuit was filed in retaliation for their service of a 60 day Notice to Quit, and the complaint itself does not so claim. Instead, the complaint pleads that Plaintiff was wrongfully evicted from her apartment in violation of the relevant statutory provisions. "Terminating a tenancy or removing a property from the rental market are not activities taken in furtherance of the constitution rights of petition or free speech." (Moriarty v. Laramar Management Corp. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 125, 137.). Furthermore, the Court finds that even if a protected activity is implicated, Plaintiffs have carried their burden to show the complaint is "both legally sufficient and supported by a sufficient prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment if the evidence submitted by the plaintiff is credited." (See Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 88-89.) Plaintiffs present evidence showing that Cindy Pon has not registered an ownership interest in the property and that she has not moved into the property in the four months since the purported owner move-in that is the subject of the lawsuit.
The Court finds the anti-SLAPP motion was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. In particular, the Court observes that Defendant's contention that the lawsuit was based on an unlawful detainer action and resulting judgment is completely and utterly without factual support, as no such action was ever filed. The Court therefore awards fees and costs to Plaintiff in the amount of $2,562.50, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5(a). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
